text: Add src/cli/ReadRequestCommand.js to implement the thin command-line read flow on top of the read client.
goal: Implement apiease read, apiease update, and apiease delete as public CLI commands.
context: Follow the existing CreateRequestCommand pattern for argument parsing, API key resolution, output selection, and exit codes.
Read should resolve API keys through ApiEaseHomeConfigurationResolver when --api-key is omitted.
steps: Parse the read command arguments needed to identify a single request and connect to APIEase.
Resolve the API key using the same explicit-flag-over-home-configuration precedence used by create.
Call the read client and render either human-readable output or raw JSON output.
constraints: Keep the command class thin and delegate all transport logic to the client.
Require an explicit request identifier argument instead of inferring it from positional input.
acceptanceCriteria: The command supports apiease read --request-id <id> --base-url <url> --shop-domain <shop-domain> with optional --api-key <api-key> and --json.
Missing required arguments fail fast with usage-level output and no API call.
Default output is human-readable and JSON mode emits the raw structured client result.
Success exits zero and failures exit non-zero.
